Ad Text:
Through the merger of Bank Australia and Qudos Bank we’re creating a better, stronger and more resilient bank for our customers and employees. As one company with two trusted brands, we’re building on the strong foundations of our history and shared values as we bring together the best of both banks.

The Technology Product Manager – Banking and Retail is accountable for the strategic ownership, investment and continuous improvement of the bank’s banking & retail technology platforms. Working closely with the customer area and business stakeholders, the role defines and delivers a clear platform roadmap aligned to business priorities, overseeing the full product lifecycle from requirements analysis through to design, build, testing, implementation and ongoing optimisation. The role ensures platforms are reliable, scalable and well governed, enabling efficient financial operations and strong business outcomes.

This role is based in Collingwood, Bendigo, Traralgon, Moe, Mascot or Canberra.

Why join us

We have big plans to become Australia’s leading purpose-driven bank.
As a certified B Corp, we’re part of a global business community who meet the highest social and environmental standards, putting purpose over profit.
Every role and person in our bank are essential to bringing our values, purpose and aspiration to life.
We offer flexible working options, competitive salary and 14% super.
Our staff have access to a range of meaningful benefits to support their physical and mental wellbeing including 24/7 free counselling, free flu vaccinations and subsidised gym memberships.
We also support staff with study assistance, paid parental leave (regardless of gender or carer responsibility), fertility treatment, volunteer, bereavement, gender transition and family violence leave, and the opportunity for bonus annual leave.
We support our staff to grow their careers through learning and development and an organisational culture where we reward and recognise innovation and values, not long hours.
We are committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce that reflects the Australian community.
We care for a conservation reserve – where we work with key partner organisations to protect threatened species and involve staff in conservation.

What you will be doing

Own the end‑to‑end technical lifecycle of banking & retail platforms, ensuring effective investment, reliability and continuous optimisation.
Partner with relevant leaders to maximise platform capability, utilisation and operational efficiency.
Define and manage product roadmaps, priorities and backlogs using insights from user needs, market trends and data.
Lead the full product lifecycle from requirements, design and build through to testing, implementation and ongoing improvement.
Apply appropriate product and delivery methodologies to balance business priorities, compliance and efficiency outcomes.
Coordinate user testing, releases, communications and training to support successful adoption and change.
Provide expert technical advice, translating strategy into operational delivery and informed leadership decisions.
Maintain and share specialist platform knowledge, drawing on internal expertise and third‑party providers.
Manage third‑party vendors, including performance monitoring, issue resolution and service improvement planning.
Deliver initiatives iteratively, ensuring stakeholders are informed on progress, risks and backlog status.
Lead and support direct reports to deliver consistent, high‑quality product outcomes.

What you will bring

Demonstrated experience in product or project management, including delivery of financial services solutions and ongoing application releases.
Tertiary qualifications in Technology, Engineering, Commerce or Finance (preferred), with experience in banking systems (e.g. Genesys, Threatmetrix, Napier etc) desirable.
Clear, confident communication and strong stakeholder‑management skills, able to influence, provide direction and support decision‑making across all levels.
Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability, including requirements gathering, gap analysis, process mapping (as‑is/to‑be), budgeting and performance tracking.
Ability to define business and data requirements, develop insights, and manage data integrity and confidentiality.
Strategic, continuous‑improvement mindset, able to adapt plans, manage competing priorities and deliver results in complex, regulated environments.
Strong understanding of banking platforms and the broader banking systems ecosystem, with capability to operate as a senior functional analyst.
Ability to translate complex technical and strategic concepts into clear, actionable outcomes for non‑technical audiences.
Experience working with third‑party vendors and representing the organisation externally.
Self‑driven, results‑focused approach with the ability to lead or contribute effectively within multidisciplinary teams.
Commitment to Bank Australia’s purpose, aspiration, values and brand as well as the B Corp ethos.

By submitting an application you agree to Bank Australia’s Privacy Policy for applicants and confirm that you are legally able to work in Australia. Bank Australia is an equal opportunity employer committed to sustainable development. We encourage people from different backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people from different cultural backgrounds and people with disabilities because we want to reflect the diversity of our communities. We are committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment process and supportive workplace.

Bank Australia reserves the right to withdraw this advertisement at any time. Candidates will be progressed as applications are received, rather than after the advertisement closure date. If you wish to be considered, please submit your application as soon as possible. Please note, the successful candidate will be subject to satisfactory background checks including but not limited to police and previous employment.

We're partnering with a leading banking client on a time-critical regulatory program driven by RBA changes, and are looking for Senior Business Analysts with strong credit cards / unsecured lending experience.

This is a true end-to-end delivery role - ideal for BAs who can operate across both business and technology and thrive in fast-paced, high-impact environments.

The Role

You'll play a key role in delivering regulatory change across the credit card lifecycle, working closely with business, product, and engineering teams.

Responsibilities include:

End-to-end business analysis across cards & lending products
Defining requirements and translating into technical solutions
Performing impact assessments, dependency mapping, and traceability
Supporting delivery across build, testing, and implementation
Working across a hybrid Agile / Waterfall environment
Driving outcomes in a time-sensitive, high-visibility program
What You'll Bring
Proven experience as a Senior BA in Retail Banking
Strong credit cards / unsecured lending knowledge (essential)
Experience across the end-to-end card lifecycle
Ability to operate across both business and technology analysis
Strong experience working in Agile delivery environments (with hybrid exposure)
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Nice to Have
Experience leveraging AI tools (e.g. Copilot) to improve delivery and productivity
Exposure to regulatory or compliance-driven programs
Why This Role
$950/day on a high-demand skillset
4 roles available - strong chance of placement
Fast 1-stage interview process
High-impact work aligned to regulatory change (RBA)
Opportunity to work on core banking products at scale

If you're a delivery-focused BA with strong cards experience, this is a standout contract to step into immediately.

We are seeking a Technical Lead to join our Agentic Automation team and work with our clients to deliver high-quality automation. You will be responsible for leading client engagements and managing a team of consultants to deliver high-quality services. You will work closely with leaders in the agentic automation division to develop and implement strategies to drive growth and profitability.

Responsibilities & tasks for this role

Lead the analysis, design, development, testing, and deployment of agentic automation solutions.

Lead and train a team of developers to deliver agentic automation solutions.

Oversee end-to-end project delivery, ensuring quality, timeliness, and client satisfaction.

Act as the primary point of contact for clients, building strong, trust-based relationships.

Identify business development opportunities and support proposal and pre-sales efforts.

Drive continuous improvement in delivery processes, methodologies, and team capabilities.

Stay current with emerging technologies (e.g., agentic AI, etc.) and translate them into client value.

Must-have qualifications & competencies

Minimum of 4 years of experience in automation

Proven track record of managing client engagements and developing strong client relationships

Knowledgeable in at least one area of consulting, such as strategy, operations, technology, or finance

Strong team leadership skills, with the ability to motivate and develop team members.

Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build and maintain strong client relationships

Bachelors degree in a relevant field, with an MBA or other advanced degree preferred

We're supporting a major Defence program with an opportunity for an EVM Specialist to join during a critical phase of program mobilisation.

This role will work across project controls, delivery teams, and stakeholders to ensure strong performance insight and reporting.

Suited to someone with strong project delivery experience and exposure to EVM within complex programs.

The highlights:

Contract Length: 12 months +
Location: Henderson (Adelaide/Melbourne considered)
Working Environment: Hybrid
Security Clearance: NV1 (essential)
The role:

Supporting multiple Defence projects as they are established and mature through early delivery phases
Working closely with the Project Controls team to support performance analysis across cost, schedule, and scope
Gathering, validating, and analysing project data to generate meaningful insights
Partnering with Control Account Managers (CAMs) and key stakeholders to understand performance drivers
Supporting preparation activities for Integrated Baseline Reviews (IBRs)
Translating complex project information into clear, actionable updates for stakeholders
Contributing to the uplift of project controls and EVM capability across the team
Experience Required:

Experience working within Defence programs (CASG exposure highly regarded)
NV1 security clearance
Background in project controls, project delivery, or program support roles
Experience with Earned Value Management (EVM) in a Defence project environment.
Experience supporting or participating in Integrated Baseline Reviews (IBRs) is highly regarded
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and communicate insights clearly
Confident stakeholder engagement skills, particularly working with technical and delivery teams



All applications should include your resume as a word document.

For more information, visit our website, www.kinexus.com.au or contact Ellen Hanratty on 03 9982 0303.

Sydney, NSW 

Key Accountabilities:

Conduct thorough interviews and workshops with stakeholders to understand the current endpoint protection and compliance policy landscape.

Identify gaps between current and desired workflows and propose solutions to bridge these gaps using Intune capabilities.

Document current and future-state business processes and workflows related to endpoint protection and compliance policy management.

Collaborate with IT teams and security experts to gather detailed technical requirements for Intune implementation.

Document integration points with existing systems and any dependencies that need to be considered.

Include risk assessment and mitigation strategies to address potential issues during migration.

Collaborate with change management teams to create a communication plan for stakeholders regarding the migration project.

Work closely with compliance officers and security teams to ensure that migrated policies meet regulatory requirements and security standards.

Provide training and support to end-users and IT teams on using Intune for endpoint protection and compliance policy management.

Share insights with other teams or departments within the organization to promote knowledge sharing and continuous improvement.

Other duties, if any, as directed by the manager 

Key Skills:

Mandatory:

Over 5 years of experience in Business Consulting/Business Analysis across multiple geographies and domains.

Proven business analysis experience, with knowledge and experience of Digital and Infrastructure projects and implementation.

Demonstrated ability to present and explain complex technical information to the developers and testers.

Good experience in keeping all project and business documents in single – SharePoint location.

Demonstrated ability to build cross-functional relationships, demonstrated high level stakeholder management and engagement skills.

Good experience in aspects of change management, including project analysis and coordinating strategic projects in a complex environment.

Conducting Requirement Gathering Workshops

Creation of Standard Operating Procedures

Creating Approach Notes, Business Proposals/Business Cases

Business Process Improvement (BPI)

Collaboration experience – stakeholder management

Documentation of artefacts including BRD, SRD, User manual, process mapping.

 Desirable: 

Strong Analytical and design skills.

First-rate planning and organization skills and experience; able to adapt and react to changing environments

Good collaboration skills and ability to work effectively in a team environment

Strong facilitator; adept at running meetings with end users, gathering, and managing business requirements, and balancing priorities

 Qualifications

Bachelor’s Degree in Information Technology or related field; or an equivalent combination of education and experience sufficient to successfully perform the key accountabilities of the job required.

Melbourne, Victoria | Hybrid Opportunity

New Opportunity | Permanent Role

Competitive Salary | Discount on RACV Products





About the Opportunity 

Reporting to the BA Practice Team Leader, the Lead Senior Business Analyst plays a pivotal role within RACV’s Program Management and Digital functions. Working across Agile, Scrum-based squads and project environments, you’ll contribute to initiatives ranging from straightforward enhancements to highly complex, enterprise-wide transformations.




In this role, you’ll partner closely with the RACV Leisure division, with a key focus on the replacement of core systems. You’ll collaborate with senior leaders and delivery teams to translate business needs into actionable solutions that create meaningful outcomes for our members, customers, and people.




Your success will be driven by strong analytical thinking, exceptional communication skills, and a pragmatic approach to problem-solving. Solid experience working in Agile environments is essential, while exposure to Adobe Experience Manager (AEM), CMS platforms, and API documentation will be highly regarded.







Come along for a new journey 

You will be instrumental in ensuring our Program Management team deliver exceptional service. Here’s a glimpse of what you’ll be doing:




Lead the BA workstream within the project, manage the workload of the Business Analysts within the project.  Provide scope management, and hands‑on delivery using established methodologies and toolsets.

Managing and mentoring a team of Business Analysts within a project, ensuring quality of outputs and alignment to project timelines.

Plan and govern BA scope across multiple workstreams, providing structured cross‑team planning and coordinating delivery sequencing to maintain alignment and momentum.

Deliver high-quality analysis artefacts by Lead scoping, planning, analysis, and documentation for BA deliverables.

Design and document use cases, user journeys, and functional behaviors required for delivery.

Contribute to shaping and refining the delivery roadmap, ensuring alignment with business priorities and strategic outcomes.

Document business processes, rules, requirements, benefits, dependencies, and risks.

Collaborate with Product Owner on backlog grooming, options analysis, and benefits realisation.

Produce high-quality epics, user stories, and acceptance criteria aligned with UX principles and Business Rules.

Conduct stakeholder analysis and facilitate workshops and Refinement Sessions

Map and document current‑state (as‑is) and future‑state (to‑be) business processes, workflows, and digital experiences.

Analyse business rules, dependencies, risks, and benefits associated with proposed changes.

Engage stakeholders across RACV (risk, compliance, legal, operations, etc.) to ensure alignment with required processes and work with support teams to create user guides and training materials.

Provide transition documentation and support knowledge transfer while sharing insights and learnings with the BA Practice.




Who we are looking for and what you will bring 

5+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst, with end-to-end project delivery.

2+ years’ experience as a lead Business analyst in a large-scale Implementation project.

Strong BA Methodology and best practice

Experience Planning and governing BA scope across multiple workstreams, providing structured cross‑team planning and coordinating delivery sequencing to maintain alignment and momentum.

Experience managing and mentoring a team of Business Analysts within a project, ensuring quality of outputs and alignment to project timelines.

Experience delivering high-quality analysis artefacts by Lead scoping, planning, analysis, and documentation for BA deliverables.

Tertiary degree in Computer Science, MIS, or related field.

Strong knowledge of Atlassian Jira & Confluence, Office 365, Visio, Figma, Miro, Google or Adobe Analytics, and draw.io.

Experience in member-based or customer-facing organisations (preferred),

Exposure to a range of initiatives in regulated, service-driven environments.

Strong stakeholder management skills including working with external offshore vendor teams. 

Experience in hospitality or leisure industry systems, ideally PMS platforms (desirable)




There is so much on offer when you join RACV 

Flexible and informal work environment.

Generous discounts on RACV insurance and roadside care.

Club Membership and discounts across RACV resorts.

Monthly updated discounts from corporate partners.




Our difference 

At RACV, we believe that maintaining a diverse and inclusive workplace is essential to our success. We want our people to feel welcomed, supported, and appreciated for who they are.

About the Company

This organisation is a well-established leader in the utilities sector, delivering critical services to a large and growing customer base. With a strong focus on innovation, sustainability, and future-ready technology, they are investing heavily in data, AI, and advanced analytics.




Their technology function is undergoing a major uplift, with AI positioned as a key driver of smarter decision-making, operational efficiency, and long-term value creation. You'll be joining a business where AI is not just experimental-it's becoming embedded across the enterprise.


About the Role

This is a senior, hands-on leadership role responsible for building and scaling enterprise AI capability. As Lead Data Engineer, you'll design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning solutions that deliver tangible business outcomes across complex operational environments.




You'll work closely with data leaders, architects, and business stakeholders to identify opportunities and bring high-impact AI use cases to life-from predictive models through to cutting-edge generative AI solutions.




Key responsibilities include:





Leading end-to-end AI/ML solution design, development, and deployment
Driving innovation across NLP, LLMs, and advanced ML techniques
Establishing and scaling MLOps practices (CI/CD, monitoring, lifecycle management)
Building on modern cloud and data platforms (Azure, Databricks)
Embedding AI into enterprise systems to improve efficiency and decision-making
Championing governance, best practice, and responsible AI frameworks
Mentoring engineers and leading cross-functional delivery teams

This role offers a strong mix of strategy and hands-on engineering, with real influence over how AI is adopted across the organisation.


About You

You're an experienced AI/ML engineer with a strong track record of delivering production-ready solutions at scale. You're comfortable operating across both technical and business domains and enjoy turning complex ideas into practical outcomes.




You'll bring:





Strong background in software or data engineering (Python preferred)
Experience with cloud platforms (Azure preferred) and Databricks ecosystem
Deep experience with ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn)
Solid MLOps capability, including model deployment and monitoring
Exposure to generative AI, LLMs, and modern AI frameworks (e.g. LangChain)
Proven ability to deliver AI solutions that drive measurable business value
Strong stakeholder engagement and leadership experience

Company description:



We're leading Western Australians to their intelligent energy future.

And we want you to be part of it.

You might know us as Western Australia's largest electricity generator and energy retailer, supporting 1.2 million customers across the state.

But what you might not know, is that we're investing in renewable energy projects, including wind farms and big batteries, to help build a more sustainable and reliable future.

Our big batteries are now live in Kwinana and Collie, we're expanding the Warradarge Wind Farm, building the new King Rocks Wind Farm, and we have many other projects in the works.

We're a purpose-driven organisation, owned by the people of WA. Our commitment is to ensure all Western Australians have access to secure, reliable, and sustainable energy.

We believe that the benefits of renewable energy aren't just for some, they're for everyone.

This is where you come in.



Job description:

Role Description:

The Senior Business Analyst is a 12 month fixed term contract in our Customer Business Unit based at our Central Park offices in Perth.

The Senior Business Analyst provides critical analytical expertise and insights to support the Customer Safeguard Program, enabling informed decision-making and efficient operational delivery. Working closely with program leads and cross-functional teams, the role ensures effective execution of customer safeguard, campaign planning, and workforce optimisation.

This position leads the analysis of customer payment data to identify trends and improvement opportunities, supporting the development of compliant, customer-focused solutions. It also ensures business requirements are clearly understood, and processes are mapped and documented so that solutions are implemented and operationalised to deliver sustainable value to the business

Job responsibilities:

Analyse business situations to identify issues and recommend improvement opportunities, identify risks associated with proposed solutions and ensure compliance with relevant policies and standards.
Facilitate stakeholder workshops to map and document business requirements and processes aligned to programme goals.
Prioritise and categorise requirements, ensuring traceability to business needs and conducting impact analysis.
Continuously build business analysis capability and share knowledge within the analysis community.
Drive process optimisation and contribute to best practice frameworks for business analysis.


Profile description:


Tertiary qualification in a related field (e.g. finance, economics, mathematics or data science)
5+ years' experience in business analysis within customer operations or Safeguard Programs.
Experience working with large datasets and reporting tools (e.g., Power BI, Excel, SQL)
Experience in energy, utilities, or regulated industries.
Familiarity with SUC obligations and customer compliance frameworks

• Learning and development opportunities.

About us

CBH is a leader in the Australian grain industry and the country's largest co-operative. Our work has meaning and is creating an exciting future - we're delivering WA's grain to the world. Our advanced logistics network stretches across the State, connecting the growers of WA with our customers, and providing opportunities for career growth in different roles and locations.

Ready to make an impact on a global scale with a local company?

We are CBH. We are what you're looking for.





The Role

The Analyst/Senior Analyst - Investment & Planning plays a key role in guiding infrastructure investment decisions across our Storage and Handling network. This role blends strong commercial assessment and modelling with the ability to streamline complex issues, connect stakeholders and drive alignment, enabling prioritised, insight‑led decision making and capability uplift across Network Planning and the wider business.

Responsibilities include:

Develop and embed best‑practice processes, tools and templates, leveraging external insights to build internal capability and support consistent delivery of the Investment Plan
Lead strategic reviews of Zones, Areas and Sites, delivering clear recommendations aligned to the Group's long‑term expansion, enhancement and sustaining capital programs
Partner with Area Managers, Operations Senior Leaders, Maintenance Superintendents and Terminal Managers to identify, assess and unlock high‑value opportunities
Draft and maintain the Group's Strategic Plan, ensuring disciplined capital deployment through long‑term network thinking, effective project prioritisation and value engineering
Undertake end‑to‑end strategic and commercial assessments for complex projects, including financial analysis, valuation and risk assessment

About You:

Bachelor's degree in Business, Commerce, Engineering or related discipline
Proven experience within either professional services, corporate strategy & planning, management consulting, Infrastructure advisory or investment banking environment
Previous experience planning and evaluating projects including modelling, preparing business cases and presenting findings and recommendations
Strong ability to create and maintain relationships with internal and external stakeholders
Knowledge of agriculture and / or alternate bulk commodities businesses (desirable)

Why work with CBH?

Work with meaning and being part of something bigger

Play a critical role in moving WA's grain from paddocks to ports, and on to countries all around the world. With an advanced logistics network and grain trading on a global level, we provide growers with first-class representation in the world's food supply chain.

We value you

CBH is a large, complex and interesting business. We value autonomy and the unique perspective you offer. The difference you make is seen and recognised.

Your time is important

We know you have family and a life outside of work and we know this is important. We provide the flexibility and work-life balance that allows you to be there for the moments that matter.

CBH offer a range of employee focused benefits aimed to encourage a healthy work life balance, including:

13-weeks paid parental leave that can be taken in a flexible way to suit unique family needs;
Free on-site gym facilities;
Ongoing training and professional development to support your growth and help you achieve your goals;
Corporate Employee discounts including travel, health insurance funds and financial services;
Employee assistance program;
Salary sacrificing scheme.

We'd love to hear from you

CBH welcomes applications from diverse backgrounds, embracing inclusivity and diversity in our workforce.

To find out more about working with CBH visit http://careers.cbh.com.au

Please note we are not accepting agency applications.

If you are already employed directly by CBH, please log in using your CBH email address or apply via our internal jobs portal.

Company description:



We're leading Western Australians to their intelligent energy future.

And we want you to be part of it.

You might know us as Western Australia's largest electricity generator and energy retailer, supporting 1.2 million customers across the state.

But what you might not know, is that we're investing in renewable energy projects, including wind farms and big batteries, to help build a more sustainable and reliable future.

Our big batteries are now live in Kwinana and Collie, we're expanding the Warradarge Wind Farm, building the new King Rocks Wind Farm, and we have many other projects in the works.

We're a purpose-driven organisation, owned by the people of WA. Our commitment is to ensure all Western Australians have access to secure, reliable, and sustainable energy.

We believe that the benefits of renewable energy aren't just for some, they're for everyone.

This is where you come in.



Job description:



The Pricing Strategy & Financial Performance Lead is an exciting opportunity in our Customer Marketing team based at our Perth Central Park office. This leadership role is critical to supporting Customer Business Unit by shaping pricing strategy, portfolio performance and financial outcomes.

You will lead a team of high performing analysts and partner closely with Sales, Products, Finance, and Trading teams to influence customer-centric decision making. Day to day you will be accountable for facilitating retail price modelling for products, retail portfolio forecasting, leading financial activities including budgeting, and guiding contestable pricing strategies across a complex and evolving market.

With a strong commercial lens, you will assess portfolio risks and opportunities, ensure robust pricing governance, and translate market, regulatory and financial analysis into clear, actionable insights for senior leaders.

This role suits a strategic minded leader who thrives with variety, dynamic environments, enjoys developing people, and is motivated by improving performance through data driven decision making, collaboration and innovation.

Job responsibilities:
Lead and develop a high performing Pricing Strategy and Financial Performance team, setting clear direction, role modelling positive culture, and ensuring delivery of high-quality commercial insights.
Drive long term financial planning for the Customer Business Unit, including demand, revenue and cost forecasting, budgeting and financial modelling, to support strategic decision making.
Define, implement and govern pricing strategies and methodologies across contestable markets and customer products, ensuring alignment with business objectives and market conditions.
Provide trusted commercial advice to senior stakeholders by analysing market, regulatory and financial impacts, identifying risks and opportunities, and translating complex data into clear, actionable insights.
Oversee portfolio and financial performance monitoring, including margin management, variance analysis and reporting, to support continuous improvement and sustainable financial outcomes.


Profile description:


Tertiary qualifications in Business, Finance, Economics or a related discipline, with strong commercial acumen and a risk aware mindset.
Proven people leadership experience, with the ability to lead, influence and develop high performing teams.
At least 3 years' experience in a senior analytics, pricing or commercial role, with demonstrated ability to interpret complex market and commercial drivers impacting profitability.
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with a track record of influencing decision making and presenting complex data clearly and concisely for non-technical audiences.
Highly developed analytical and problem-solving skills, with experience proactively proposing and driving initiatives that improve financial performance; experience with Power BI and tools such as Python or R is desirable.


We offer:

Job Title: Guidewire Program Delivery Lead - Australia and New Zealand
Ad Text:
Tenzing is a leading Guidewire consultancy across Australia and New Zealand. A Guidewire Partner since 2007, we deliver complex, integrated insurance solutions for public and private organisations across both self‑managed platforms and Guidewire Cloud.
We were recognised as the Guidewire APAC Consulting Partner for Outstanding Market Growth (2023/2024). Our current work includes large‑scale Guidewire Cloud migrations, greenfield implementations, and long‑running DevOps delivery teams. As part of the Tech Mahindra Group, we combine strong local consulting expertise with global scale, supported by a Guidewire Centre of Excellence in India.

About the role:

We are seeking experienced Program Delivery Leads to join our delivery teams across Australia and New Zealand.

You will bring strong program leadership experience across Agile and hybrid delivery environments, with a proven ability to lead complex, multi‑stream transformation programmes. Hands‑on Guidewire delivery experience is advantageous but not essential.

This role is accountable for end‑to‑end program delivery, building high‑performing teams, and ensuring successful outcomes for both clients and delivery teams.


What You’ll Be Responsible For:


Providing end‑to‑end leadership for complex business and technology transformation programmes, including Guidewire implementations and enhancements.

Leading the delivery of large, multi‑stream programmes using Agile, SAFe, and hybrid delivery approaches.

Acting as a servant leader, creating a supportive environment that enables teams to perform at their best.

Providing day‑to‑day leadership for cross‑functional delivery teams, including developers, testers, business analysts, architects, and onshore/offshore resources.

Owning delivery planning, forecasting, and execution across sprints, releases, and milestonesActing as the primary point of accountability for delivery, proactively managing scope, dependencies, risks, and issues.

Building and maintaining strong, trusted relationships with client stakeholders, sponsors, and product owners to ensure alignment on priorities and outcomes.

Ensuring delivery aligns with agreed solution architecture, Guidewire best practices, and cloud standards.

Facilitating and governing Agile ceremonies (planning, reviews, retrospectives) and embedding effective ways of working.

Managing vendor, partner, and offshore delivery contributions to ensure seamless integration and performance.

Tracking and reporting delivery progress, financials, and risks, providing clear and transparent updates to senior stakeholders.

Championing continuous improvement, delivery excellence, team wellbeing, and strong governance across the programme




Experience & Background:

You will bring:

Proven program or senior delivery management experience within complex transformation environments.

A track record of successfully delivering large, multi‑stream programs using Agile, SAFe, or hybrid methodologies.

Strong leadership presence, with the ability to build and motivate high‑performing teams across diverse roles and backgrounds.

Excellent communication, negotiation, and risk management skills.

Experience delivering insurance or enterprise technology platforms (Guidewire experience is advantageous).




How You’ll Work:
At Tenzing, we value strong technical capability alongside excellent consulting and delivery skills. You’ll demonstrate:

Consulting and customer‑focused mindset - professional, clear communicator with a strong focus on delivering meaningful business outcomes and value for customers.

Agile delivery and collaboration - proven experience working in Scrum or SAFe environments, collaborating effectively with cross‑functional teams and stakeholders.

Clear communication and influence - able to explain complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.

Ownership, quality, and problem solving - takes accountability for outcomes, applies critical thinking to solve problems, and cares about building high‑quality, reliable, and maintainable solutions.

Adaptable and future‑focused - resilient in changing environments and proactively adopts AI‑enabled tools to improve individual and team productivity.


Why Join Tenzing?
For over twenty years, Tenzing has helped organisations navigate complex change. Our Sherpa ethos means standing alongside our clients, doing the hard work with them, and guiding them to successful outcomes.
As an independent subsidiary of the Tech Mahindra Group, we offer the best of both worlds - access to global expertise and opportunities, while maintaining our strong local culture and ways of working across New Zealand and Australia.
We invest in our people through tailored career development, mentoring, and a supportive, inclusive environment. Our leaders are experienced, approachable, and committed to helping our teams grow and thrive.


What We Offer:

Competitive salary

Discretionary annual bonus

Flexible and hybrid working arrangements

Option to purchase additional annual leave

Fully subsidised life and income protection insurance

Discounted medical insurance

Comprehensive learning and development programmes

Wellbeing initiatives including EAP and flu vaccinations

Inclusive culture with regular social events and celebrations


If you’re ready to work on meaningful Guidewire projects, grow your expertise, and build your career in a supportive consulting environment, we’d love to hear from you.

Our client is a well-regarded consulting partner delivering Modern Workplace and Microsoft 365 solutions across enterprise and government environments. They are known for their strong delivery capability, high standards in governance and compliance work, and their ability to support complex digital transformation programs across regulated industries.
 
Their teams operate in a collaborative consulting environment where technical depth, stakeholder engagement and practical delivery outcomes are equally valued.
 
The Opportunity

This is a Mid to Senior level Microsoft 365 Consultant role sitting within a growing Modern Workplace practice. You will play a key role in delivering end-to-end Microsoft 365 solutions across a range of enterprise and government clients.
 
The role offers exposure to multiple high-impact engagements, with a strong focus on governance, compliance and information management within the M365 ecosystem. It is suited to someone who enjoys client facing consulting work and wants variety across different environments rather than a single internal platform role.
 
This opportunity is available on an initial 6-month contract with extension options, or potentially a permanent appointment depending on preference and fit.
 
The Role

You will be responsible for designing, implementing and optimising Microsoft 365 environments, with a strong focus on secure, scalable and well governed solutions.
 
Key responsibilities include:
Solution design and delivery

Design and implement Microsoft 365 solutions across SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, OneDrive for Business and Exchange Online
Develop scalable and secure architectures aligned to client requirements
Contribute across the full project lifecycle from discovery through to delivery and optimisation
Governance, compliance and information management

Design and implement Microsoft 365 governance frameworks
Develop retention policies, labels and data classification models
Deliver records management solutions using Microsoft Purview
Support clients with compliance and information lifecycle management requirements
Stakeholder engagement and consulting

Engage with business and technical stakeholders to gather and define requirements
Run workshops and translate business needs into technical solutions
Provide clear guidance on Microsoft 365 capability and best practice adoption
The Ideal Person

You are a strong Microsoft 365 Consultant with experience delivering in enterprise or government environments. You are confident working in client facing settings and can translate complex technical concepts into clear, practical solutions for stakeholders.
 
You will bring proven experience delivering Microsoft 365 solutions in enterprise and/or government, ideally within regulated environments. You have a strong grounding in governance, compliance and information management, and you are comfortable working in client-facing Modern Workplace consulting roles.
 
Technically, you are hands-on across SharePoint Online, Teams, OneDrive and Exchange Online, with Microsoft Purview experience spanning retention, compliance and records management. You also understand how to apply Microsoft 365 security and governance controls to deliver secure, scalable and well-governed outcomes.
 
You communicate clearly and can simplify technical concepts for a range of stakeholders. Job Title: Guidewire Business Analyst – Australia and New Zealand

At Senior level, mentoring and supporting junior business analysts and contributing to standards and continuous improvement initiatives.

Guidewire & Domain Experience:

Hands‑on experience as a Business Analyst working on Guidewire implementations or enhancements.

Familiarity with Guidewire InsuranceSuite and Guidewire Cloud concepts.

Experience translating business processes into functional and user‑story‑based requirements.

Strong understanding of insurance domains (policy, claims, and/or billing), particularly at Senior level.

Adaptable and future‑focused - resilient in changing environments and proactively adopts AI‑enabled tools to improve individual and team productivity.

About Us

Adaptiv is a true Kiwi success story! Recognised for our commitment to premium client delivery, we provide world-class integration, AI consulting and development service, along with data and analytics. We have a wealth of development and integration experience in moving clients from around the world onto cloud platforms.




Our integration work focuses on the exciting challenge of designing innovative API-led and event driven solutions. Leveraging the latest technologies, such as Databricks, MuleSoft, Boomi, Azure, and Solace, we develop services that unlock access to existing data within legacy business systems in new and ingenious ways.




These solutions enable organizations to drive major process efficiencies, engineer new operational workflows, and extract deeper insights from their data. Ultimately, this empowers businesses to operate more effectively and engage with their customers in modern, cutting-edge ways. Our services also include cloud consultancy, round-the clock middleware support, integration development, application lifecycle management, load and performance testing and customised integration training.




Based in our stylish Southbank offices in Melbourne, our team enjoys a collaborative, people-first culture where curiosity, initiative, and growth are valued. We combine serious technical capability with a down-to-earth, can-do approach and we’re looking for someone who shares that mindset.





About the Role

We have an exciting opportunity for a Senior Data Engineer / Consultant to join our growing Data Engineering practice here at Adaptiv Integration to drive the delivery of data engineering engagements on behalf of our clients. In this role, you will ensure high-quality technical outcomes, foster strong client collaboration, and provide effective support to delivery teams.




This position combines advanced data engineering expertise with consulting leadership. You will own engineering delivery across assigned engagements, contribute to solution design within architectural guidelines, and mentor junior and intermediate team members to maintain consistent quality and standards.




This role will be responsible for the delivery of cutting-edge Databricks technology, driving innovative data engineering solutions for clients. You will leverage your expertise to design, implement, and optimise Databricks-based pipelines and solutions, ensuring high-quality technical outcomes and efficient, scalable data architectures.




The role is highly hands-on and delivery-focused, requiring autonomous execution and a strong emphasis on billable contribution.




What You’ll Be Doing

Build, maintain, and optimise data pipelines across lakes, lakehouses, and warehouses.
Write, test, and maintain high-quality code; develop transformations for reporting, analytics, and AI/ML.
Apply data modelling principles; troubleshoot and resolve issues efficiently.
Work with Databricks, Delta Live Tables, Lakeflow, Git, and CI/CD pipelines.
Implement automated testing, data validation, and performance optimisation.
Lead unit and integration testing; ensure solutions meet quality and documentation standards.
Support knowledge sharing and improvement of internal frameworks and templates.
Act as primary engineering contact, leading technical discussions and translating business needs into technical tasks.
Communicate progress, risks, and blockers; contribute to estimation, scoping, and planning.
Mentor and coach junior/intermediate team members; conduct peer reviews.
Ensure accountability for code quality, documentation, and adherence to best practices.
Collaborate with architects on technical solutions and delivery planning.
Participate in learning sessions and knowledge sharing; adopt modern data concepts (AI/ML, data visualisation, cloud-native).
Contribute to process, template, and documentation improvements; pursue relevant training and certification.




What You’ll Bring

6+ years of professional experience in data engineering or a related field.

At least 4 years of hands-on experience developing data pipelines and transformations using Databricks, including Delta Live Tables, notebooks, and workflows.

Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric concepts and experience contributing to projects that incorporate Fabric components.

Proficiency in Python and SQL for data transformation and analysis.
Understanding of data modelling including Star and Snowflake schema design for analytics workloads.
Knowledge of Medallion architecture principles and well-architected, scalable data solution design.

Experience applying DevOps practices such as version control, CI/CD, and environment automation.

Experience with testing and data quality frameworks to ensure reliable, accurate data delivery.

Experience with visualisation and data consumption concepts, including Power BI or similar tools.


Relevant industry certifications (Databricks Professional and Databricks Associate) or a demonstrated commitment to ongoing professional development.

·         Lead the research agenda for a university with globally recognised research strengths and a bold new Strategic Plan

·         Shape a research culture and support environment that is ambitious, inclusive and built for the future

·         Drive transformational change in how UNE enables its research community to thrive

The University of New England is setting out to do things differently. This role is no exception. Regionally rooted but globally networked, UNE is home to research that matters — knowledge that connects regional Australia to national and global opportunity, and that supports individuals and communities to thrive. With a new Strategic Plan setting an ambitious direction, this is an exciting moment to join us.

The Role

As Director of Research, you will shape and sustain the strategic and operational effectiveness of UNE's research support environment. Reporting to the Deputy Vice-Chancellor Research (DVC-R), you will provide authoritative counsel on research strategy, policy, governance and compliance, while leading a directorate that is collaborative, responsive and continuously improving.

This is a role that exists to remove barriers, build confidence and create the conditions in which UNE's researchers can do their best work — from early-career academics finding their feet, to established research leaders pursuing ambitious programs.

It is also a role for someone who can lead through change. UNE is in the midst of an operating model transition, and the Director of Research will be expected to actively contribute to new ways of working — embracing simplification, AI-enabled workflows and capability uplift as genuine priorities, not afterthoughts.

What You'll Lead

·         Research strategy development and implementation in partnership with the DVC-R, including shaping institutional research priorities and managing the Research Performance Framework

·         A capable, service-focused Research Services directorate

·         The full research development lifecycle. From grant identification and application support through to reporting, acquittal and post-award management

·         A research culture that is ambitious, inclusive and well-supported, including mentoring early-career researchers and building capability across the institution

·         External partnerships with funding bodies, government agencies, industry and peer institutions, with particular focus on opportunities of regional significance

·         Institutional leadership on research governance, integrity, ethics, compliance, export controls and foreign interference obligations

·         Operational excellence. Reducing administrative burden and improving the researcher experience

·         Thoughtful integration of AI and digital tools to improve research support outcomes

What You'll Bring

·         Significant experience in research leadership, development and administration at a senior level, with a track record of building institutional research capacity and culture

·         A doctoral qualification (preferred) or equivalent senior experience in research management and strategy

·         Deep understanding of the Australian research funding landscape, regulatory environment and compliance obligations

·         Demonstrated ability to lead diverse teams, foster collaboration across academic and professional boundaries, and build productive external partnerships

·         Strategic and entrepreneurial thinking in research support — identifying opportunities, managing risk and driving continuous improvement

·         An appreciation for data-informed research management, including familiarity with research performance frameworks or researcher productivity systems

·         Highly developed communication, relationship-building and influencing skills, with the ability to operate credibly across all levels of the institution and with external stakeholders

·         A genuine capacity to contribute to organisational change, including the adoption of AI-enabled tools and evolving governance frameworks

If you're energised by the prospect of building something genuinely better, in an institution with the ambition and appetite to match, we'd love to hear from you.

Why UNE?

UNE has always punched above its weight, in research quality, in regional impact, and in its willingness to do things differently. This is a rare opportunity to help write the next chapter: a research environment that is ambitious, modern and genuinely enabling — for a leader who acts with courage, builds relationships that last, and delivers results that matter.

About the University of New England

The University of New England (UNE) is a regionally based, globally networked university renowned for the quality of its student experience and the excellence of its research specialisations. It stands as a beacon of innovation, with a legacy stretching back to the 1950s when it became Australia’s first provider of distance learning. Today, UNE continues to redefine remote learning through cutting-edge programs and a deeply student-centred approach.

UNE’s education model combines unrivalled experience with modern, flexible delivery, ideal for students juggling life, work, or remote locations. With a library of over 200 courses, robust support systems, and a recognised track record for quality, UNE offers more than access to education, it offers a future-focused learning journey designed for success in a rapidly evolving world.

UNE emphasises its people, staff and students' growth and development. We aspire to be a leader in innovation and change and a centre for the Armidale and the North-West communities.

Equity, diversity and inclusion are central to the core values of UNE, and we are committed to engaging, supporting and growing a richly diverse workforce. From the Oorala Aboriginal Centre at the heart of our campus, to the inclusive organisations such as the UNE Ally Network, the UNE Women’s Society, and the many international cultural groups - all will feel valued and respected at UNE.

Commitment to Inclusion and Diversity

UNE is committed to building a diverse workforce and strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander Australians, people with disability, people from diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds, mature age workers and LGBTQIA+ people.

About Armidale

Armidale is an attractive and vibrant city that combines all the features of a bustling regional town centre with the tranquility of rural living. It boasts gourmet restaurants and cafes, boutique shopping, quality schools and sporting facilities. Surrounded by mountain gorges, waterfalls and national parks, Armidale’s natural beauty and distinct seasons make it an ideal place to live and thrive. To find out more, visit https://www.visitarmidale.com.au/experience

Job Title: Sessional Lecturer – Information Technology - Canberra Campus
Ad Text:
About Wentworth Institute of Higher Education (WIN) 

Wentworth Institute of Higher Education (WIN) is a growing, TEQSA-registered higher education provider with campuses in Sydney and Canberra, offering undergraduate and postgraduate programs across business, information technology, interactive media, and accounting. As we continue to expand, we are building a strong academic team focused on high-quality teaching, student engagement, and industry-relevant learning outcomes. Students are central to everything we do, and we are committed to a supportive, structured, and academically rigorous environment that enables both students and staff to succeed. 

About the role 

Location: Canberra

Employment type: Sessional (Casual appointment) 

Appointment: Semester-based 

This role is focused on teaching and assessment delivery, working within an established subject structure with clear coordination and academic support. 

We are particularly seeking expertise in: 

Programming (e.g. Python, C#, PhP) 

Databases and Data Management (Relational, and NoSQL) 

Project Management 

Software Testing and Quality Assurance 

Industry experience and familiarity with contemporary IT practice are highly regarded, particularly where candidates can bring applied insights and current technologies into the classroom. 

Key responsibilities 

Deliver structured, engaging teaching aligned with subject learning outcomes 

Teach in accordance with approved subject outlines and assessment requirements 

Assess student work fairly and consistently using approved criteria and rubrics 

Provide timely, practical, and constructive feedback to support student progression 

Participate in moderation and academic review processes 

Maintain academic integrity in assessment design and delivery 

Engage professionally with subject coordinators and academic leadership 

Use institutional learning systems and comply with relevant academic policies 

About you 

You bring: 

A Master’s degree (or higher) in Information Technology or a closely related discipline, with a PhD or progress towards a PhD highly regarded 

Demonstrated ability to deliver structured teaching and assess student work effectively at tertiary level 

Experience in higher education teaching and/or relevant industry experience, with the ability to apply this effectively in the classroom 

The ability to integrate theory with contemporary practice in a way that is relevant to student learning 

Strong judgement in assessing student work in a fair, consistent, and transparent manner using defined criteria 

The ability to engage effectively with a diverse, international student cohort 

A clear understanding of academic standards, including assessment integrity and quality expectations 

Support and development 

At WIN, sessional staff are supported through: 

A structured teaching environment with clear subject coordination and defined expectations 

Access to academic leadership and guidance throughout the teaching period 

Orientation to learning systems, assessment requirements, and moderation processes 

Ongoing development aligned to teaching quality and scholarship of teaching and learning 

How to apply 

Please submit: 

A brief cover letter outlining your teaching areas and relevant experience, and indicating the subject areas you are able to teach 

A current CV 

Appointments are made based on teaching needs and subject availability, with teaching allocations aligned to upcoming teaching periods. 

Applications close 29 May 2026. 

Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted. 

For enquiries about the role, please contact Dr Sai Lakkaraju at sai.lakkaraju@win.edu.au

About the Role

A NSW Government agency is seeking an experienced Power BI Visualisation Specialist to join a high-performing data and research team on a short-term contract through to end of June 2026, with a strong likelihood of extension into the new financial year.

This is a front-end enhancement role on a live, flagship government digital platform. The dashboards are already built - your job is to refine, polish and elevate them based on a ready-to-implement backlog of design and UX improvements.

What You'll Be Doing

- Enhancing existing Power BI dashboards based on design team feedback.
- Refining colour palettes, chart layouts, visual hierarchies and report formatting.
- Improving overall dashboard UX and presentation standards.
- Collaborating with a small team of economists, designers and communications staff.
- Supporting platform users and incorporating feedback into ongoing improvements (if extended).

What You Won't Be Doing

This is a pure front-end role, no backend data work, no calculations, no data structure changes. If you love clean, well-designed BI reporting and want to focus purely on the visual layer, this is the role for you.

What We're Looking For

- Strong hands-on experience developing and refining Power BI dashboards.
- A strong eye for design, layout and data visualisation best practice.
- Ability to work autonomously and manage a backlog of improvements.
- Good communication skills and comfort working across a multi-disciplinary team.
- High level of integrity and professional judgement - the platform integrates sensitive government and commercial data.

Contract Details

- Rate: $85/hr + super
- Location: Haymarket, Sydney CBD
- Working arrangement: Hybrid - minimum 1 day in office per week, ideally 3 (negotiable)
- Hours: 35 hours per week (7 hours/day)
- Start: ASAP
- End: 30 June 2026, with likely extension into FY27

About the Role

The Finance & Strategy (F&S) team is dedicated to financial reporting and enabling budget execution and optimization to deliver growth and efficiencies across the company. This role serves as a key Business Partner to the Go-To-Market (GTM) organisation across ANZ.

Identify opportunities to build and streamline processes, policies, and tools — including AI-powered solutions — to enable efficiency and scale

Build positive relationships with key partners across the business

An exciting opportunity is available for a highly skilled Operational Technology Cyber Security Business Analyst to join a forward‑thinking public sector organisation in Brisbane. This pivotal role is ideal for someone who enjoys delivering meaningful outcomes by shaping technology initiatives across OT and cyber security environments.

You will be central to initiatives that enhance security, efficiency, and compliance across critical infrastructure, working closely with internal stakeholders and delivery partners to support seamless OT and cyber security implementations. The organisation offers a supportive, inclusive workplace with flexible working arrangements and ongoing professional development, enabling you to grow your career while making a genuine impact.

Why join?

Enjoy flexible working arrangements that support work-life balance
Collaborate with experienced, values-driven teams in a supportive and inclusive environment
What you'll do

You will lead structured discovery activities, engage stakeholders across the organisation, and translate business needs into clear, testable requirements aligned with industry best practice.

Key skills and experience include:

Demonstrated experience leading structured discovery activities in OT or cyber security settings
Proven capability performing gap analyses against IS18, ISO27000, or similar frameworks
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including facilitating workshops across multidisciplinary teams
Experience developing well-structured business cases for executive decision-making
High attention to detail when producing project initiation and requirements documentation
Advanced capability in producing testable, traceable requirements across the project lifecycle
Experience working with vendors and maintaining alignment through traceability frameworks

A proactive, collaborative mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and risk mitigation
Strong process analysis and redesign skills to enhance operational efficiency, security, and resilience
